overview.line: Through and group winners already.
A point against South Africa sends them through.
Must beat Mexico and hope the other match falls right.
Must beat Korea Republic and overturn goal difference.

Mexico.text: Through to the knockout stage as group winners.
First place is already locked; the final match against Czechia changes nothing. As winners they meet a third-placed team in the round of 32.

Korea Republic.text: A draw or a win against South Africa secures a top-two finish.
Self-sufficient: the result against South Africa decides it, whatever happens between Czechia and Mexico.

breakdown.text: Win: through, and second place secured.
Draw: still through on four points.
Lose: drop out of the top two, with only a best-third place left.

Korea Republic.text: If a draw leaves them level with Czechia, the 2-1 win over Czechia settles it in Korea Republic's favour.
Should they lose, three points is often enough for a best-third spot, but on one point it would be borderline and dependent on other groups.

Czechia.text: Alive, but only with a win over Mexico.
A win is not self-sufficient: it also needs the South Africa–Korea Republic result to cooperate.

breakdown.text: Win: in the mix for second, but the other match and goal difference can still bump them to third.
Draw: out of the top two.
Lose: out of the top two.

Czechia.text: In a three-way tie on points, goal difference becomes the decider, and that can still swing on the final scorelines.
A third-place finish would depend on other groups; on one or four points the lean is no better than even.

South Africa.text: Alive, but needs to beat Korea Republic.
A win is not enough on its own; goal difference and the Czechia–Mexico result matter.

breakdown.text: Win: into contention for second, decided on goal difference.
Draw: out of the top two.
Lose: out of the top two.

South Africa.text: Best-third qualification would hinge on other groups and looks a stretch on the points available.

overview.line: A point against Switzerland guarantees top two.
A point against Canada guarantees top two.
Needs to win and overturn a heavy goal-difference gap.
Needs to win big and hope the top two slip.

Canada.text: A draw with Switzerland is enough to go through.
The Switzerland match is decisive; a point clinches it.

breakdown.text: Win: through and top of the group.
Draw: through in second at worst.
Lose: relies on the parallel result and goal difference.

Canada.text: The Canada–Switzerland result decides first place directly.

Switzerland.text: A draw with Canada is enough to go through.
Mirror of Canada: a point secures a top-two place.

breakdown.text: Win: through and top of the group.
Draw: through in second at worst.
Lose: exposed to the other result and goal difference.

Switzerland.text: If the top two are level, Canada's +6 goal difference leads Switzerland's +3, so a heavy result would be needed to overturn it.

Bosnia and Herzegovina.text: Mathematically alive, but needs to beat Qatar and see the leaders drop points.
Only a win keeps any chance open, and even then goal difference is a steep climb.
A best-third route would need other groups to fall short; on the current points it is unlikely.

Qatar.text: A long shot: needs a big win over Bosnia and Herzegovina and favours elsewhere.
Only a sizeable win, combined with the leaders dropping points, keeps any path alive.

overview.line: A point against Scotland guarantees top two.
A point against Haiti guarantees top two.
Beat Brazil to go through; anything less leaves it tight.
Eliminated; cannot reach the top three.

Brazil.text: A point against Scotland secures a top-two finish.
The Scotland match decides it; a draw is enough.

breakdown.text: Win: through, in first.
Draw: through, second at worst.
Lose: relies on Morocco's result and goal difference.

Brazil.text: First place is contested with Morocco; the head-to-head was a 1-1 draw, so position turns on the final results.

Morocco.text: A point against Haiti secures a top-two finish.
A draw against Haiti is enough; a win likely takes top spot.

breakdown.text: Win: through, pushing for first.
Draw: through, second at worst.
Lose: out of the top two unless results elsewhere help.

Scotland.text: Beat Brazil to be sure of going through.
A win secures top two; anything less leaves them exposed.

breakdown.text: Win: through.
Draw: out of the top two, best-third only.
Lose: out of the top two, best-third only.

Scotland.text: On three points a best-third place is often enough, but it depends on how other groups finish.

Haiti.text: On zero points Haiti cannot climb out of fourth: even a win over Morocco leaves them behind Scotland on the head-to-head, so no top-three finish is possible.

overview.line: Through and group winners already.
Beat Paraguay to be sure; cannot finish lower than third.
Beat Australia to be sure; cannot finish lower than third.
Eliminated; cannot reach the top three.

United States.text: Through to the knockout stage as group winners.
First place is already locked; the match against Türkiye does not change it.

Australia.text: A win over Paraguay guarantees second place.
The Paraguay match is decisive and self-sufficient on a win.

breakdown.text: Win: through in second.
Draw: third, into the best-third race.
Lose: out of the top two.

Australia.text: If it finishes third on four points, qualification is likely but still hangs on other groups.

Paraguay.text: A win over Australia guarantees second place.
Mirror of Australia: win and they are second.

breakdown.text: Win: through in second.
Draw: third, into the best-third race.
Lose: out of the top two.

Paraguay.text: A draw leaves third on four points, a strong but not certain best-third position.

Türkiye.text: On zero points Türkiye lost to both Australia and Paraguay, so even a win over the United States leaves them last on the head-to-head. No top-three finish remains.

overview.line: Through and group winners already.
A point against Curaçao sends them through.
Must beat Germany and hope the other match falls right.
Must beat Côte d'Ivoire and overturn goal difference.

Germany.text: Through to the knockout stage as group winners.
First place is already locked; the match against Ecuador does not change it.

Côte d'Ivoire.text: A draw or win against Curaçao secures a top-two finish.
Self-sufficient: their own result against Curaçao decides it.

breakdown.text: Win: through in second.
Draw: still through.
Lose: out of the top two.

Côte d'Ivoire.text: Should they lose, one point would leave best-third qualification unlikely and dependent on other groups.

Ecuador.text: Alive, but only by beating Germany.
A win is not self-sufficient; the Curaçao–Côte d'Ivoire result also matters.

breakdown.text: Win: into the second-place contest, decided on the other match and goal difference.
Draw: out of the top two.
Lose: out of the top two.

Ecuador.text: A points tie for second would fall to goal difference, which the final scorelines can still move.

Curaçao.text: A long shot: needs to beat Côte d'Ivoire and erase a -6 goal difference.
Only a win keeps any chance open, and goal difference makes it a steep climb.

overview.line: A point against Tunisia guarantees top two.
A point against Sweden guarantees top two.
Beat Japan to go through; anything less leaves it tight.
Eliminated; cannot reach the top three.

Netherlands.text: A point against Tunisia secures a top-two finish.
The Tunisia match decides it; a draw is enough.

breakdown.text: Win: through, pushing for first.
Draw: through, second at worst.
Lose: relies on the Japan–Sweden result and goal difference.

Netherlands.text: First place is shared with Japan on points and goal difference, so the final matches decide the order.

Japan.text: A point against Sweden secures a top-two finish.
A draw with Sweden is enough; a win pushes for first.

breakdown.text: Win: through, pushing for first.
Draw: through, second at worst.
Lose: out of the top two unless results elsewhere help.

Sweden.text: Beat Japan to be sure of going through.
A win secures top two; anything less leaves them exposed.

breakdown.text: Win: through.
Draw: out of the top two, best-third only.
Lose: out of the top two, best-third only.

Sweden.text: On three points a best-third place is often enough, but it depends on how other groups finish.

Tunisia.text: On zero points and a -8 goal difference, Tunisia cannot reach the top three even by beating the Netherlands.
